problem_statement: AI-generated drafts only become part of the product if approved recipes can flow into the existing recipe library and planning system. A disconnected draft object would force duplicate code paths and make the feature feel temporary rather than integrated.
in_scope: Map approved AI recipe drafts into the existing recipe and ingredient persistence flow
Support save recipe and save plus add to plan actions using validated draft data
Ensure pantry ingredient references are stored in a way that remains compatible with current scoring and planning logic
out_of_scope: Database schema changes for AI provenance
Bulk save of multiple drafts at once
requirements: Approved drafts must reuse the current recipe creation path or equivalent shared insert logic
Saved AI recipes must appear in existing recipe lists and plan selection flows without special-case rendering
Save failures must not leave partial recipe records behind
acceptance_criteria: A reviewed AI draft can be saved into recipes and recipe_ingredients without schema changes
A saved AI draft is visible through the current recipe library and plan flows after refresh or revalidation
Save plus add to plan creates the recipe and a corresponding meal plan entry only after explicit user action
dependencies: P2-ai-03 AI recipe draft review surface
risks: Loose ingredient mapping may reduce the quality of later pantry scoring if AI ingredient names drift too far from pantry naming
Combining save and plan actions could create duplicate writes if idempotency is not handled carefully
implementation_notes: Prefer extending shared recipe insert helpers in src/actions/recipes.ts rather than duplicating persistence logic. Keep all AI saves behind explicit confirmation.
